findings.description: The RE: line positioning differs - in the PDF it is right-aligned/centered on the right side, while in the HTML it appears left-aligned
findings.evidence: PDF shows 'RE: Aligned Certification Periods...' positioned toward the right side of the page; HTML shows it left-aligned

findings.description: The 'Attention:' label and its content formatting differs slightly - in PDF 'Attention:' is on the same line with indented content, in HTML it appears as a separate label with content below
findings.evidence: PDF has 'Attention:' followed by tabbed/indented text on same line; HTML has 'Attention:' as part of the bold text block

findings.description: The signature block layout differs - PDF has signatures arranged in a specific two-column layout with more spacing, HTML approximates this but with slightly different positioning
findings.evidence: PDF shows signatures with specific spacing and alignment; HTML renders them in a similar two-column arrangement but with slightly different visual treatment

findings.description: Ron Byrd's signature in the HTML appears to have a highlighted/background box around it that is not present in the PDF
findings.evidence: PDF shows Ron Byrd's signature as a plain handwritten signature; HTML shows it with what appears to be a dark background rectangle

findings.description: Dean Simpson's signature in the HTML appears to have a visible border/box around it that is not present in the PDF original
findings.evidence: PDF shows Dean Simpson's signature without a border; HTML shows it with a visible rectangular border

findings.description: The document uses h1 for the department name but lacks any sub-headings (h2, h3, etc.) for structural elements like the RE line, attention line, or the positive potential section. While not a broken hierarchy, the flat structure doesn't fully reflect the document's organization.
findings.evidence: Only one h1 exists: 'North Carolina Department of Health and Human Services'. The RE line, bullet section headers, and signature block have no heading tags.

findings.description: The HTML contains nested <main> elements, with an outer <main> wrapping the entire content and an inner <main> wrapping the letter body. This is an HTML structural issue but does not cause content duplication.
findings.evidence: HTML has <main> at the top level and another <main> nested inside after the <header> block.

findings.description: The link to www.ncwss.com is preserved and accurate. The HTML renders it with an http:// prefix which is a reasonable normalization.
Alt text for images is reasonable and descriptive, correctly identifying signatures and the state seal.
findings.evidence: Alt texts: 'North Carolina State Seal', 'Signature of Dean Simpson', 'Signature of Carolyn McClanahan', 'Signature of Chris Urso', 'Signature of Ron Byrd'
